content:
Q: Are there any companies excluded from adopting the audit exemption framework?

A: Yes, subsidiaries of public companies, exempt private companies which have
opted to lodge certificate relating to their status as an exempt private
companies, and foreign companies are excluded.

section_title: Can dormant companies adopt the audit exemption framework?
content:
Q: Can dormant companies adopt the audit exemption framework?

A: Yes, companies that have been dormant since incorporation or dormant in
the current financial year and in the immediate past financial year qualify for
audit exemption.

section_title: What happens if a company loses its eligibility for exemption?
content:
Q: What happens if a company loses its eligibility for exemption?

A: If a company no longer meets the criteria, it will lose its eligibility to adopt
the audit exemption framework for future financial years but shall remain
exempt for the qualifying years in which it is so exempt.

section_title: What documents must a company lodge to the Registrar if it elects for audit exemption?
content:
Q: What documents must a company lodge to the Registrar if it elects for audit exemption?

A: The company must lodge unaudited financial statements, a directors' report,
and a certificate of compliance within 30 days of circulation pursuant to
Section 254 of the Companies Act 2016 (CA 2016).

section_title: Is audit exemption framework applicable to a private company which is a subsidiary of a public company?
content:
Q: Is audit exemption framework applicable to a private company which is a subsidiary of a public company?

A: Generally, the law requires every company to appoint an auditor for each
financial year. However, under Section 267(2) of the CA 2016, the Registrar is
empowered to exempt certain categories of private companies from having to
appoint an auditor for a financial year.
Nonetheless, even if the company is a private company where it is a
subsidiary of a public company, the subsidiary company will not be eligible
for audit exemption.

section_title: If a company has corporate shareholders and meets the criteria, can they enjoy the company audit exemption?
content:
Q: If a company has corporate shareholders and meets the criteria, can they enjoy the company audit exemption?

A: For a company to elect to be exempted from audit, it must be a private
company and must satisfy the criteria applicable to the company to qualify
for the audit exemption. A private company which has corporate shareholders
but fulfils the criteria can enjoy the audit exemption for the relevant years.

section_title: Can a private exempt company that elects to lodge a certificate relating to its status as an exempt private company (EPC
content:
Q: Can a private exempt company that elects to lodge a certificate relating to its status as an exempt private company (EPC) with SSM choose to elect to be exempted from audit?

A: No. An exempt private company cannot elect to be exempted from an audit
if it chooses to lodge an EPC certificate with SSM in lieu of financial
statements. However, an exempt private company is eligible to elect for audit
exemption provided the company satisfies the criteria and lodges its
unaudited financial statements with SSM.

section_title: Do I need to apply for audit exemption and what is the form to complete?
content:
Q: Do I need to apply for audit exemption and what is the form to complete?

A: Similar like exempt private companies, you need not apply for audit
exemption with SSM. A company is eligible for audit exemption if it satisfies
the criteria set in PD 10/2024 and if it elects to do so. The company can
always appoint an auditor to conduct an audit if there is a need and the
company is not prohibited from engaging one.

section_title: How do companies determine their number of employees?
content:
Q: How do companies determine their number of employees?

A: The number of employees is based on the number of full-time employees
employed by the company at the end of each relevant financial year.
For the purpose of PD10/2024, full-time employees mean paid workers
working for not less than 6 hours a day for at least 20 days a month or
working for at least 120 hours a month.
PART Q - Page 5
Full-time workers include local, foreign, contract workers and workers
undergoing probationary period but excluding-
(a) a director who is also working as a full-time employee;
(b) a shareholder who is also working as a full-time employee; or
(c) family members or friends who are unpaid or receiving irregular wages
while working in the company.

section_title: How does a company that satisfies the new audit exemption criteria elect for the first time not to have an audit after t
content:
Q: How does a company that satisfies the new audit exemption criteria elect for the first time not to have an audit after the PD 10/2024 takes effect?

A: For a company that qualifies and wishes to elect for an audit exemption, the
company must first assess its audit exempt financial period commencing after
the exemption takes effect to see whether it fulfils the requirements set in
the said PD for the current and immediate past periods.

section_title: Certain companies are unable to take advantage of the audit exemption because certain agencies (e.g. financial instituti
content:
Q: Certain companies are unable to take advantage of the audit exemption because certain agencies (e.g. financial institutions, government agencies and other related authorities) still require companies to submit audited financial statements for various purposes. Does SSM have any comment on this?

A: SSM's policy intent to introduce audit exemption is to reduce regulatory and
financial burden of certain companies. It also aims to support SMEs growth
aligned with the current legal requirements and international best practices
relating to financial reporting which focuses on assisting small and mediumsized enterprises.

content:
Q: A. What does "must not exceed the maximum threshold specified for the respective corresponding phase" in paragraph 9 of PD10/2024 mean? B. If we assess a company's audit exemption status in 2026, should we apply the 2026 threshold (RM2 million revenue/assets and 20 employees) to the years 2024 and 2025? Or should we use the earlier thresholds (RM1 million revenue/assets and 10 employees) for those years? (Updated 19 March 2025)

A: The phrase means that when assessing a company's eligibility for audit
exemption, its revenue, assets and number of employees must remain
within the set limits for each relevant phase.

section_title: Does PD10/2024 have a retrospective effect on audit exemption eligibility? (Updated 19 March 2025)
content:
Q: Does PD10/2024 have a retrospective effect on audit exemption eligibility? (Updated 19 March 2025)

A: No, PD 10/2024 does not have a retrospective effect. The directive applies
only to financial years starting on or after 1 January 2025.
For financial years beginning on or before 31 December 2024, the PD 3/2017
remains applicable. Companies that qualify for audit exemption under
PD3/2017 will remain exempted for those financial years.
PD10/2024 increased the thresholds in phases over 3 years, but it does not
alter past audit exemption decisions made under PD3/2017.

part: SCENARIO 6
section: SCENARIO 6
section_title: Does a private company that is an associate of, or jointly controlled by, a public company eligible to adopt Practice Di
content:
Q: Does a private company that is an associate of, or jointly controlled by, a public company eligible to adopt Practice Directive No. 10/2024 (PD 10/2024)? (Updated 6 November 2025)

A: A private company which is an associate of a public company is eligible to
adopt PD 10/2024 if it fulfils all the relevant criteria set-out in the PD
10/2024.
However, an audit is required to be carried out if the company receives a
notice in writing from members or the Registrar requiring the company to
audit its accounts during the financial year pursuant to paragraph 14 of PD
10/2024 and section 267 of the CA 2016.
A private company which is part of a public company either as a subsidiary
or through joint control, is deemed to have greater public interest is not
eligible to adopt PD 10/2024.

part: SCENARIO 6
section: SCENARIO 6
section_title: Does PD10/2024 still applicable if there are other requirements, laws or guidelines which require financial statements t
content:
Q: Does PD10/2024 still applicable if there are other requirements, laws or guidelines which require financial statements to be audited? (Updated 6 November 2025)

A: PD10/2024 is not applicable when there are other requirements, laws or
guidelines that mandate the auditing of a company's financial statements. In
such circumstances, the requirements imposed based on the relevant
requirements, laws or guidelines shall prevail.

part: SCENARIO 6
section: SCENARIO 6
section_title: Is a newly incorporated private company that is not dormant eligible for audit exemption immediately upon meeting the pr
content:
Q: Is a newly incorporated private company that is not dormant eligible for audit exemption immediately upon meeting the prescribed thresholds? (Updated 6 November 2025)

A: No. Under PD 10/2024, eligibility for audit exemption requires a company to
meet the thresholds for the current financial year and has financial
information for the immediate past 2 financial years. Since a newly
incorporated company that is not dormant does not have 2 preceding years
of financial data, it does not qualify for audit exemption.

part: SCENARIO 6
section: SCENARIO 6
section_title: If a private holding company consolidates the results of its private subsidiaries and the consolidated financial stateme
content:
Q: If a private holding company consolidates the results of its private subsidiaries and the consolidated financial statements exceed the thresholds at Group level, will the private holding company and its subsidiaries still qualify for audit exemption when the holding company and subsidiaries meet the thresholds individually? (Updated 6 November 2025)

A: To determine the eligibility of a company to adopt PD 10/2024, the criteria
are applied to the financial results of the company at company's level.
Therefore, the private holding company and private subsidiaries still qualifies
for audit exemption if both companies individually meet the threshold even
though consolidated financial statements exceed the threshold at Group
level.
Similarly, if the consolidated financial statements of the private holding
company and private subsidiaries do not satisfy the conditions set out in PD
10/2024 but financial statements of either the private holding company or
private subsidiaries at company level satisfy these conditions, the holding
private company or the private subsidiaries still qualify for audit exemption.

section_title: Given that 2 commonly applied accounting standards in Malaysia are MPERS and MFRS, which may result in differences in th
content:
Q: Given that 2 commonly applied accounting standards in Malaysia are MPERS and MFRS, which may result in differences in the reported value of total assets: (i) Can either accounting standard be used to determine whether a company meets the thresholds for audit exemption? (ii) If the immediate preceding year's financial statements (prepared under MPERS) qualified for audit exemption, but the current year's audited financial statements are prepared under MFRS and include comparative figures showing that the thresholds were exceeded in the prior year, would the company be required to submit audited financial statements for the prior year? (Updated 6 November 2025)

A: (i) Yes. The choice of accounting standards (MPERS or MFRS) will not affect
the determination of company's audit exemption thresholds, as the
assessment is based on prescribed financial criteria such as revenue,
assets and number of employees.
(ii) No. The company will not be required to submit audited financial
statements for the prior year if it had already qualified for audit
exemption at that point of time. Comparative figures presented under
PART Q - Page 13
MFRS do not retrospectively alter the exemption status of the prior year.

section_title: Who is responsible for determining company's eligibility to apply for audit exemption? the company itself or a licensed
content:
Q: Who is responsible for determining company's eligibility to apply for audit exemption? the company itself or a licensed auditor? (Updated 6 November 2025)

A: The company is responsible for making its own assessment of eligibility. In
addition to meeting the prescribed criteria, the company must also ensure
that it is not subject to any liabilities or third-party obligations that require
the preparation or submission of audited financial statements.

section_title: For a newly incorporated or existing company that qualifies for audit exemption under PD 10/2024 and opts to prepare una
content:
Q: For a newly incorporated or existing company that qualifies for audit exemption under PD 10/2024 and opts to prepare unaudited financial statements, would it constitute an offence under the Companies Act 2016 if no auditor is appointed? (Updated 6 November 2025)

A: The CA 2016 does not prescribe any offence for a company that is eligible
and opts for audit exemption if no auditor is appointed. The company may
determine, based on its own needs and circumstances, the need to appoint
an auditor.

section_title: When a company that qualifies for audit exemption under PD 10/2024 has appointed an auditor beforehand, must the auditor
content:
Q: When a company that qualifies for audit exemption under PD 10/2024 has appointed an auditor beforehand, must the auditor be removed, terminated, or resign before the company can proceed with the preparation and filing of unaudited financial statements with SSM? (Updated 6 November 2025)

A: There is no requirement under the CA 2016 or PD 10/2024 for the appointed
auditor to be removed, terminated or to resign before the company prepares
and files unaudited financial statements. The decision to retain or remove
PART Q - Page 14
the auditor rests entirely with the company. However, the company must
ensure that all eligibility criteria under PD 10/2024 are fully met, including
satisfying the prescribed thresholds.
